# UC-22.2.44 · NIS2 Cooperation Group and Sector Information Sharing Participation

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Beginner &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch both your meeting follow-through for industry cooperation groups and whether fresh safety tips are still flowing in from trusted partners. If meetings stall or the tip line goes quiet, we flag it so leaders can nudge the right people.*

---

## Description

Correlates the cooperation-forum register with closed ServiceNow meeting tasks and STIX indicator volume per named feed so lapses in sector ISAC participation or silent intel pipelines become visible.

## Value

Public affairs and cyber leaders can demonstrate active collaboration and functioning information exchanges without maintaining separate spreadsheets, which matters when policymakers ask for proof of collective resilience investments.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain `nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv` with `group_id` aligned to ServiceNow `assignment_group` and intel `feed_name`. (2) Ingest STIX via ES TAXII or HEC. (3) Tune `feed_floor` per forum. (4) Monthly CISO review. (5) Store exports in `index=audit_evidence`.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.2.44` results, associated `snow:task` references, summarized `stix` volume stats, and archived dashboard PDF in `index=audit_evidence`.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Simulate a forum with `participation_gap=1` by closing the ServiceNow task in `Resolved` instead of `Closed` and confirm the search lists the forum; separately zero-out STIX events for a feed and verify the low `indicators_consumed` branch fires when under the configured floor.

### Negative scenario

When the latest task is `Closed` within cadence and thirty-day `stix` volume exceeds the forum-specific floor, the dashboard stays empty for that `group_id`, showing the negative path for healthy participation.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- This UC implements NIS2 Art.14 — Cooperation Group provisions and Art.29 — voluntary cyber security information-sharing arrangements. NIS2 requires essential and important entities to participate in sector-specific information sharing and analysis centres (ISACs), national cooperation groups, and ENISA-facilitated coordination forums. Participation has two measurable dimensions: (1) meeting attendance and follow-through (process evidence), and (2) consumption of shared threat intelligence (technical evidence). This UC monitors both dimensions.
- **Why this matters for NIS2 compliance**: Art.14(4)(e) requires cooperation groups to "exchange best practices and information in relation to the implementation of this Directive." Art.29 states that entities "may, on a voluntary basis, exchange relevant cybersecurity information, including information relating to cyber threats, near misses, vulnerabilities." An entity that is a registered member of an ISAC but never attends meetings and never processes the shared indicators is not meeting the spirit of NIS2. This UC provides the evidence to demonstrate active participation.
- **Governance lookup**: `nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv` with columns: `group_id` (unique identifier matching both the ServiceNow `assignment_group` name and the STIX `feed_name`, e.g., "EU-ENERGY-ISAC", "NO-NCSC-SECTOR", "FIRST-EU-SIG"), `group_name` (human-readable name), `meeting_cadence_days` (expected meeting frequency — 90 for quarterly, 30 for monthly, 180 for biannual), `liaison_email` (the organisation's designated representative), `feed_type` (TAXII, email, portal — how indicators are received), `classification` (TLP:WHITE, TLP:GREEN, TLP:AMBER — determines handling restrictions), `nda_required` (yes/no), `alt_evidence` (yes/no — if "yes", meeting evidence comes from outside ServiceNow and manual attestation is accepted).
  This CSV is maintained by the CISO office. It encodes the organisation's entire information-sharing landscape. Validate: `| inputlookup nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv | stats count dc(group_id) as forums dc(classification) as tlp_levels values(feed_type) as feed_types`. Expected: `forums` matches the number of ISACs and cooperation groups listed in the organisation's NIS2 registration filing.
- **Threshold lookup**: `nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds.csv` with columns: `group_id`, `feed_floor_threshold` (minimum expected unique indicators per 30 days), `exception_until` (ISO 8601 date for temporary exemptions). The `feed_floor_threshold` varies dramatically by feed: a national CSIRT STIX feed may produce 5,000+ indicators per month, while a niche sector ISAC may produce 50. Set the threshold at ~50% of the observed 90-day average to catch pipeline failures without triggering on normal volume fluctuations.
- **ServiceNow integration**: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) configured to pull the `task` table (`/api/now/table/task`) into `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task`. The UC searches for tasks with `short_description` containing "ISAC" or "cooperation group" — these represent meeting coordination tasks. Key fields: `assignment_group` (maps to `group_id`), `state` (New, In Progress, Closed, Resolved — only "Closed" indicates complete follow-through), `closed_at` (timestamp of task closure). If your organisation uses a different ITSM system (Jira, BMC Remedy), adapt the subsearch accordingly.
  Note: ServiceNow task `state` semantics: "Resolved" means the assignee considers the work done but it hasn't been verified. "Closed" means verified and accepted. For NIS2 evidence purposes, only "Closed" counts — it proves the meeting actions were followed through, not just attended.
- **STIX/TAXII threat intelligence**: Splunk Enterprise Security's Intelligence Downloads feature (Enterprise Security > Configure > Data Enrichment > Intelligence Downloads) supports TAXII 2.0/2.1 feeds. Each ISAC typically provides a TAXII server URL, a username/API key, and a collection ID. Events are stored as `index=threat_intel sourcetype=stix` with fields: `indicator_id` (unique STIX indicator identifier, e.g., `indicator--<uuid>`), `feed_name` (the source feed, mapped to `group_id`), `indicator_type` (ipv4-addr, domain-name, file:hashes, etc.), `valid_from`, `valid_until`. For ISACs that share indicators via email (PDF reports with IOCs) rather than TAXII, use a manual process: extract IOCs and POST to HEC as `sourcetype=stix` with the correct `feed_name`.
- **Index retention**: `index=itsm` ≥ 2 years (meeting evidence). `index=threat_intel` ≥ 1 year (indicator lifecycle). `index=audit_evidence` ≥ 7 years.
- **RBAC**: role `nis2_cooperation_analyst` with `srchIndexesAllowed = itsm, threat_intel, audit_evidence`. Threat intelligence data (especially TLP:AMBER indicators from ISACs) is highly sensitive and subject to NDA — restrict access strictly. The cooperation group register itself may reveal which forums the organisation participates in, which could be operationally sensitive.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
**ServiceNow task data:**
In the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow, verify the `task` table input is active:
- Data Inputs > ServiceNow > Table: `task`
- Filter: `assignment_group LIKE ISAC OR assignment_group LIKE cooperation` (optional, reduces volume)
- Poll interval: 300 seconds
- Index: `itsm`
- Sourcetype: `snow:task`

Verify:
```
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task (short_description="*ISAC*" OR short_description="*cooperation group*") earliest=-180d
| stats count dc(assignment_group) as groups values(assignment_group) as group_list
| eval group_list=mvjoin(group_list, ", ")
```
Expected: `groups` matches the number of active forums in your register. `group_list` should contain recognisable ISAC/cooperation group names. If `count=0`, the task table input may not be pulling cooperation-related tasks — check that the tasks are created in ServiceNow with the correct `short_description` keywords.

**STIX/TAXII intelligence feeds:**
For each ISAC in the register that provides a TAXII feed:
1. In ES: Configure > Data Enrichment > Intelligence Downloads > New
2. URL: the ISAC's TAXII server URL (e.g., `https://taxii.eu-energy-isac.org/api/v1/taxii2/`)
3. Authentication: API key or username/password as provided by the ISAC
4. Collection: the specific collection ID (some ISACs have multiple collections for different TLP levels)
5. Interval: 3600 seconds (hourly)
6. Type: TAXII 2.0 or TAXII 2.1

Verify per feed:
```
index=threat_intel sourcetype=stix feed_name="EU-ENERGY-ISAC" earliest=-30d
| stats dc(indicator_id) as unique_indicators count as total_events earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen
| eval first_seen=strftime(first_seen, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"), last_seen=strftime(last_seen, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
```
Expected: `unique_indicators` should be above the `feed_floor_threshold` from the threshold lookup. If zero, the TAXII feed is not delivering — check ES Intelligence Downloads health (the status indicator should be green).

**For ISACs without TAXII (email/portal-based):**
Create a lightweight process: when the CISO office receives a PDF with IOCs, extract them (manually or with a script) and POST to HEC:
```json
{
  "event": {
    "indicator_id": "indicator--manual-2026-04-001",
    "feed_name": "NO-NCSC-SECTOR",
    "indicator_type": "ipv4-addr",
    "indicator_value": "198.51.100.42",
    "valid_from": "2026-04-15T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "sourcetype": "stix",
  "index": "threat_intel"
}
```
This ensures the UC can track indicator consumption even for non-automated feeds. The `feed_floor_threshold` for manual feeds should be much lower (e.g., 10-50 per month).

**Lookup configuration:**
Place both CSVs in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-nis2-cooperation/lookups/`. Define in `transforms.conf`:
```ini
[nis2_cooperation_group_register]
filename = nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv
case_sensitive_match = false

[nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds]
filename = nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
```

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save as `UC-22.2.44 NIS2 Cooperation Group Participation`.

#### Understanding this SPL — design decisions:

- **`inputlookup` as the driver**: the cooperation group register is the authoritative list of forums the organisation is committed to participating in. Using it as the driver ensures that forums with ZERO ServiceNow activity and ZERO STIX indicators are still surfaced — the most critical finding (complete disengagement from a forum).

- **Two `join type=left` blocks**: the SPL performs two independent enrichments:
  - **First join (ServiceNow tasks)**: finds the latest task state and closure date for each forum. `latest(state)` and `latest(closed_at)` give the most recent meeting follow-through evidence. If no tasks exist for a forum, the join returns null — the `participation_gap` eval then flags this as a gap.
  - **Second join (STIX indicators)**: counts unique indicators consumed per feed in the last 30 days. If the feed has gone silent (pipeline broken, TAXII credentials expired, ISAC discontinued the feed), `indicators_consumed` will be zero or null — flagged against the `feed_floor_threshold`.

- **`participation_gap=if(last_meeting_state!="Closed" OR isnull(last_meeting_date),1,0)`**: a forum has a participation gap if either (a) the latest meeting task was not closed (left in "Resolved" or "In Progress" — meaning follow-through is incomplete), or (b) there is no meeting task at all (the organisation hasn't logged any ISAC meeting activity in ServiceNow in the last 180 days).

- **`feed_floor_threshold=coalesce(feed_floor_threshold,1000)`**: default threshold of 1,000 unique indicators per 30 days if no forum-specific threshold is configured. This default is appropriate for large national CSIRT feeds. For smaller ISACs, override with a lower value in the threshold lookup.

- **Exception register**: `where isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d")` filters out forums that have a current exception (e.g., "summer recess until August 31" or "feed migration in progress until May 15"). Expired exceptions are automatically re-included.

Schedule: cron `0 9 * * 1` (weekly, Monday at 09:00). This is a governance-level check, not an operational alert — weekly cadence is appropriate. Time range: embedded in the SPL (180d for ServiceNow, 30d for STIX). Trigger: `Number of results > 0`.

Alert actions: (1) Email to the CISO with the list of forums showing participation gaps or low indicator consumption. (2) For forums with `participation_gap=1` AND `meeting_cadence_days < 90` (i.e., monthly meetings being missed): escalate to the liaison email. (3) Write all results to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=nis2:cooperation:evidence`.

Extended search — comprehensive participation scorecard:
```spl
| inputlookup nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv
| join type=left group_id
    [search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task (short_description="*ISAC*" OR short_description="*cooperation group*") earliest=-365d
     | stats count as meetings_logged latest(closed_at) as last_closed dc(assigned_to) as participants by assignment_group
     | rename assignment_group as group_id]
| join type=left group_id
    [search index=threat_intel sourcetype=stix earliest=-30d
     | stats dc(indicator_id) as indicators_30d dc(indicator_type) as indicator_types by feed_name
     | rename feed_name as group_id]
| fillnull meetings_logged value=0 indicators_30d value=0 participants value=0
| eval days_since_last=if(isnotnull(last_closed), round((now()-strptime(last_closed,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/86400,0), 999)
| eval meeting_status=case(
    days_since_last > meeting_cadence_days*2, "OVERDUE",
    days_since_last > meeting_cadence_days, "DUE_SOON",
    1==1, "ON_TRACK")
| lookup nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds group_id OUTPUT feed_floor_threshold
| eval feed_floor_threshold=coalesce(feed_floor_threshold, 1000)
| eval feed_status=case(
    indicators_30d=0, "SILENT",
    indicators_30d < feed_floor_threshold, "LOW",
    1==1, "HEALTHY")
| table group_id group_name meeting_status days_since_last meetings_logged participants feed_status indicators_30d indicator_types
| sort meeting_status feed_status
```
This scorecard provides a complete view of each forum's health: meeting cadence, participant diversity, intel feed volume, and indicator type diversity.

### Step 3 — Validate
(a) **Meeting gap detection**: in ServiceNow, find a cooperation group task that is in "Resolved" (not "Closed") state. Run the search. Confirm the forum appears with `participation_gap=1`. Close the task in ServiceNow. Wait for the next Splunk Add-on poll (5 minutes). Run the search again. Confirm the forum no longer appears.

(b) **Missing meeting evidence**: for a forum with `alt_evidence=yes` in the register (meeting evidence comes from outside ServiceNow), verify that the search correctly flags `participation_gap=1` (because no ServiceNow tasks exist). This is expected behaviour — the CISO should provide a manual attestation for these forums.

(c) **STIX feed floor breach**: identify a STIX feed with low volume. Check: `index=threat_intel sourcetype=stix earliest=-30d | stats dc(indicator_id) by feed_name | sort count`. For the lowest-volume feed, set `feed_floor_threshold` in the threshold lookup to be above its actual count. Run the search. Confirm the forum appears with `indicators_consumed < feed_floor_threshold`.

(d) **Silent feed detection**: if a TAXII feed has been disabled (temporarily or accidentally), `indicators_consumed` should be 0. Verify: temporarily disable one Intelligence Download in ES. Wait 24 hours. Run the search. Confirm the forum appears with `indicators_consumed=0`.

(e) **Exception register test**: add a `group_id` to `nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds.csv` with `exception_until` set to tomorrow. Run the search. Confirm the forum does not appear. Set `exception_until` to yesterday. Run again. Confirm it reappears.

(f) **Cross-check with ES Intelligence Dashboard**: navigate to ES > Security Intelligence > Threat Intelligence. For each ISAC feed, compare the ES-reported indicator count with the `indicators_consumed` from the UC. They should match closely (±2% due to deduplication differences).

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard (recommended name: `NIS2 Cooperation & Information Sharing`):
- **Row 1 — Programme health**: Single-value `Active forums` (total count from register). Single-value `Forums with gaps` (red ≥ 1). Single-value `Total indicators consumed (30d)` (sum across all feeds). Single-value `Average meeting cadence compliance %` (percentage of forums ON_TRACK).
- **Row 2 — Participation scorecard**: table from the extended search with colour-coding: green for ON_TRACK/HEALTHY, amber for DUE_SOON/LOW, red for OVERDUE/SILENT. Drilldown on `group_id` to show: all ServiceNow tasks for that forum (meeting history) and all STIX indicators from that feed (indicator type breakdown).
- **Row 3 — Intel feed health**: `index=threat_intel sourcetype=stix earliest=-90d | timechart span=1d dc(indicator_id) by feed_name limit=10`. Line chart showing indicator consumption per feed over 90 days. Sudden drops indicate feed failures. Gradual declines may indicate the ISAC reducing output.
- **Row 4 — Meeting timeline**: `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task (short_description="*ISAC*" OR short_description="*cooperation group*") earliest=-365d | table _time assignment_group short_description state assigned_to | sort -_time`. Shows the last 12 months of cooperation activities — useful for audit evidence.

Runbook (owner: CISO / Public Affairs):
1. Weekly: review the alert. For each forum with a gap:
   - `participation_gap=1` AND no tasks in 180 days: contact the liaison and verify membership status. Is the forum still active? Has the liaison changed roles? Create a ServiceNow task for the next meeting.
   - `indicators_consumed < feed_floor_threshold`: check ES Intelligence Downloads health. If the feed is active but volume is genuinely low, lower the threshold. If the feed is broken, fix the TAXII connection (credentials, URL, certificate).
2. Monthly: present the scorecard to the CISO. Highlight: forums approaching overdue status, feeds with declining volume, and any new ISACs the organisation should consider joining.
3. Quarterly: archive the participation evidence package: dashboard PDF, ServiceNow task export, STIX volume summary, and any manual attestations for `alt_evidence=yes` forums. Sign with RFC3161.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting

- **`assignment_group` doesn't match `group_id`.** ServiceNow uses spaces and special characters in group names (e.g., "EU Energy ISAC Working Group"), while the CSV uses hyphenated identifiers (e.g., "EU-ENERGY-ISAC"). Fix: normalise in the SPL before joining: `eval group_id=replace(upper(assignment_group)," ","_")` and use the same convention in the CSV. Alternatively, add a `servicenow_group_name` column to the CSV and join on that instead.

- **TAXII feed returns HTTP 401.** The ISAC rotated API credentials and didn't notify the organisation. ES Intelligence Downloads shows "Authentication Failed" in the health status. Fix: obtain new credentials from the ISAC liaison, update ES Intelligence Downloads configuration, verify with a manual `curl` request: `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" https://taxii.isac.example/api/v1/taxii2/`.

- **ServiceNow API returns HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests).** The Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow is polling too aggressively. Fix: increase the polling interval from 300 to 600 seconds in the TA's input configuration. For the `task` table specifically, consider adding a filter to only pull cooperation-related tasks (reduces payload size and API calls).

- **`feed_name` in STIX events doesn't match `group_id` in the register.** The TAXII collection name used by ES as `feed_name` is a technical identifier (e.g., "taxii-collection-abc123") while the register uses a human-readable name. Fix: create a mapping lookup `isac_feed_map.csv` with `taxii_feed_name, group_id` and add a lookup step in the STIX subsearch: `| lookup isac_feed_map.csv taxii_feed_name AS feed_name OUTPUT group_id | rename group_id as feed_name`.

- **Duplicate indicator_id from multiple TAXII collections.** Some ISACs publish the same indicators in multiple collections (e.g., one for IOCs, one for context). The `dc(indicator_id)` count is inflated. Fix: add `| dedup indicator_id` before the `stats` in the STIX subsearch. This ensures each indicator is counted once regardless of how many collections it appears in.

- **Forum with `alt_evidence=yes` always shows `participation_gap=1`.** This is expected behaviour — meeting evidence for this forum is not in ServiceNow. The runbook should document that the CISO provides a manual attestation for these forums. To suppress the alert, either: (a) add the forum to the exception register with a rolling `exception_until` date, or (b) add a filter in the SPL: `| where NOT (alt_evidence="yes" AND participation_gap=1)` to exclude forums with alternative evidence from the participation gap check.



## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv
| lookup nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds group_id OUTPUT feed_floor_threshold exception_until
| eval feed_floor_threshold=coalesce(feed_floor_threshold,1000)
| join group_id type=left [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task (short_description="*ISAC*" OR short_description="*cooperation group*") earliest=-180d
    | stats latest(state) as last_meeting_state latest(closed_at) as last_meeting_date by assignment_group
    | rename assignment_group as group_id
]
| eval participation_gap=if(last_meeting_state!="Closed" OR isnull(last_meeting_date),1,0)
| join group_id type=left [
    search index=threat_intel sourcetype=stix earliest=-30d
    | stats dc(indicator_id) as indicators_consumed by feed_name
    | rename feed_name as group_id
]
| eval indicators_consumed=coalesce(indicators_consumed,0)
| where participation_gap=1 OR indicators_consumed < feed_floor_threshold
| where isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| table group_id, last_meeting_state, last_meeting_date, indicators_consumed, participation_gap, feed_floor_threshold
```

## Visualization

Participation gap table, timeline of meetings, single value active STIX feeds.

## Known False Positives

• **Summer holiday quorum gaps** — Distinguish: meeting task still `Work in Progress` but charter allows August pause. Suppress: `lookup nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds` lowering `feed_floor` or pausing alerts for 30 days.
• **Intel feed renamed in TAXII server** — Distinguish: `feed_name` mismatch yields zero `indicators_consumed`. Suppress: update register CSV; time-bound exception register entry.
• **Confidential side channel minutes** — Distinguish: official attendance kept outside ServiceNow. Suppress: manual attestation flag `alt_evidence=true` on register row with review ticket.
• **Synthetic test STIX drop** — Distinguish: purple-team feed spikes then goes quiet. Suppress: filter `where feed_name!="purple_team_qa"` or use exception lookup.

- [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)
